species: CEP128 is involved in spermatogenesis in humans and mice
tissue: we found that CEP128 is predominantly expressed in the mouse testis
cell_type: defective sperm flagella in both homozygous Cep128 KO and KI mice
disease_state: the male homozygous KO mice were sterile after the mating period
sample_source: Genomic DNA was isolated from peripheral blood samples of the patients
age: 1000 healthy men (25–35 years old) who had undergone medical check-ups
cell_line: we further constructed Flag-wild-type (WT)-CEP128 and Flag-CEP128R222Q plasmids and transfected them into 293T cells
sex: the male homozygous KO mice were sterile after the mating period
strain: Testicular tissue for proteomic analysis was obtained from 8-week-old C57BL/6 male mice
